generation.method: exact react npm tarball plus local Fast React package copied into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per target, scenario, and mode
generation.pathNormalization: temporary extraction roots and local workspace paths are normalized before artifact write

worker097Checked.source: generated fastReactComparisons in this oracle artifact; default Fast React act is still a placeholder and react-server act is absent

probeModes.reason: Default Node development is the only React root mode with callable public act behavior.
Default Node production records public act absence on the CommonJS root facade.
The react-server condition records public act absence on the server-safe React root facade.
Production react-server behavior is checked separately because it loads production condition branches.

captures: function name, length, and own-key order when callable
callback call count, this value, and argument count
synchronous callback return forwarding through the act thenable
no warning for unawaited rootless sync act
no no-await warning when the act thenable is awaited
development warning for unawaited async act
warning deduplication within a loaded React module
absence behavior in production and react-server modes
subsequent call behavior after synchronous throw
function values with callable then properties forwarded as sync return values

@fast-react/react.source: local workspace package copied for public React.act behavior comparison

intentionalGaps.reason: React DOM test-utils and react-test-renderer wrappers own renderer-backed act queue flushing evidence.
The probe uses only the public React root export and does not inspect ReactSharedInternals.actQueue.
Local Fast React comparisons are recorded only as unsupported, known mismatch, or matched-without-claim evidence.

args.value: You called act(async () => ...) without await. This could lead to unexpected testing behaviour, interleaving multiple act calls and mixing their scopes. You should - await act(async () => ...);
